items.title: SpaceX’s two lead underwriters have a $1 trillion chasm between their valuations as IPO quiet period ends
items.description: The two lead underwriters on SpaceX’s initial public offering, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion as they both initiated coverage at buy.

items.title: The trade of the year is entering a new phase, says Goldman Sachs. Here’s what it means for stocks.
items.description: Goldman Sachs says the HALO trade has worked well this year, but a tougher phase relying on earnings performance lies ahead.

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.description: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”

items.title: American workplaces are hostile to workers caring for aging family members. These companies are doing things differently.
items.description: Offices are about to be full of employees who have another job taking care of a loved one. Some companies are trying to adjust.

items.title: Condos are getting cheaper — but list prices don’t include this fast-growing hidden cost
items.description: New lending requirements, rising insurance premiums and aging buildings have contributed to the rising cost of condo ownership.

items.title: Russia’s oil refineries are burning — and, now, so is its bond market
items.description: Fuel shortages in Russia may achieve what nothing — and nobody — else could: force Putin to the negotiating table.

items.title: The Nasdaq-100 has been far more volatile than the S&P 500. Now add SpaceX to the mix.
items.description: While SpaceX is due to join the Nasdaq-100 on Tuesday, it won’t become part of the S&P 500 for at least another year — likely furthering the volatility spread between those two indexes.

items.title: ‘I’ve plenty of time on my hands’: Advisers bombard me with offers of free steak dinners. Is it wrong to go for the food?
items.description: “I get hungry for a meal at a good restaurant.”

items.title: The bull market got a new lease on life — can it last?
items.description: The market-timers’ bullishness is a mile wide but only an inch deep

items.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
items.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”
